description: Lock corrected chronology and removed false dependencies for foundational Classical nodes audited on 2026-06-15.

checks.reason: The broad early astrolabe node should not require the source-backed Ptolemaic model node.
Classical sculpture predates formal Alexandrian anatomical dissection.
Hippocratic clinical observation did not require systematic human dissection.
Humoral theory is a medical-philosophical model, not a child of Alexandrian anatomy.
Libraries can be tablet or scroll collections and predate codices.
Pilotage is broader than lighthouse-assisted harbor approach.
The library node is anchored to the seventh-century BCE Ashurbanipal collection.
The codex node is anchored to early codex fragments and the first/second-century transition.
The harbor lighthouse node is anchored to the Pharos of Alexandria around 280 BCE.
The pilotage node is anchored to surviving fourth-century BCE pilot books/periploi.
The Ptolemaic geocentric model is anchored to the Almagest around 150 CE.
Syllogistic is anchored to Aristotle's Prior Analytics around 350 BCE.
Roman window glass is anchored to the early imperial period.
Libraries organize written records; philosophy is possible content, not a prerequisite technology.
Early libraries can be tablet collections in existing palace/archive contexts and should not require Greek monumental construction.
Military field medicine should not require the later formal diagnosis node for basic battlefield treatment.
Broad siege engineering includes Assyrian siege engines and battering rams before Greek torsion artillery; torsion springs belong to later catapult artillery, not the broad prerequisite set.
Physical glass or rock-crystal lenses can predate formal Greek optics; early optics is explanatory theory, not a prerequisite for the object technology.
